hanging.title: Hanging
description: Vulnerable to capture while unprotected, under-protected, or under threat from a
piece or pawn - such that the opponent would gain a material advantage from its capture and the resulting exchange
(if any).
Also used to refer to leaving a winning
or a checkmate available for the opponent to play.

loose.title: Loose
description: Lacking a direct defender, and typically considered
if threatened by another pawn or piece. Loose pieces may still be
The phrase "
" is shorthand for the common
principle that loose pieces are particularly vulnerable to
such as
and it is therefore good practice to minimise the amount of pieces left loose in a given position unless
there is solid justification for their positioning.

eval_swing.title: Evaluation Swing
description: Represented by the
symbol, an evaluation swing measures the difference in chess engine evaluation from one board position to
another.
A swing of 0 would mean that both positions are theoretically equivalent, with
or
both indicating that the latter position is better than the former (for White and for Black, respectively)
roughly to the extent of 1 extra pawn.
It is intended for use as a heuristic, to provide an idea of how the benefits and drawbacks of a particular
move (as detailed in the surrounding commentary) weigh up against each other, or to compare two possible options
for moves in a given position.

value: The values provided alongside move commentary are pre-calculated, typically using Stockfish
at a depth of 25 or greater.
